__category_info__.description: Filters for remeshing, simplifying, and reconstructing 3D models.

meshing_isotropic_explicit_remeshing.description: Perform a explicit remeshing of a triangular mesh, by repeatedly applying edge flip, collapse, relax and refine operations to regularize size and aspect ration of the triangular meshing.

iterations.name: Iterations
iterations.description: Number of iterations of the remeshing operations to repeat on the mesh.

selectedonly.name: Remesh only selected faces
selectedonly.description: If checked the remeshing operations will be applied only to the selected faces.

targetlen.name: Target Length
targetlen.description: Sets the target length for the remeshed mesh edges.

featuredeg.name: Crease Angle
featuredeg.description: Minimum angle between faces of the original to consider the shared edge as a feature to be preserved.

checksurfdist.name: Check Surface Distance
checksurfdist.description: If toggled each local operation must deviate from original mesh by [Max. surface distance]

maxsurfdist.name: Max. Surface Distance
maxsurfdist.description: Maximal surface deviation allowed for each local operation

splitflag.name: Refine Step
splitflag.description: If checked the remeshing operations will include a refine step.

collapseflag.name: Collapse Step
collapseflag.description: If checked the remeshing operations will include a collapse step.

swapflag.name: Edge-Swap Step
swapflag.description: If checked the remeshing operations will include a edge-swap step, aimed at improving the vertex valence of the resulting mesh.

smoothflag.name: Smooth Step
smoothflag.description: If checked the remeshing operations will include a smoothing step, aimed at relaxing the vertex positions in a Laplacian sense.

reprojectflag.name: Reproject Step
reprojectflag.description: If checked the remeshing operations will include a step to reproject the mesh vertices on the original surface.

generate_resampled_uniform_mesh.description: Create a new mesh that is a resampled version of the current one using the marching cube algorithm over a uniform volumetric representation.

cellsize.name: Precision
cellsize.description: Size of the cell. Smaller cells give better precision at a higher computational cost. Halving the cell size builds a volume 8 times larger.

offset.name: Offset
offset.description: Offset of the created surface. If zero (represented by 50%), the created surface passes on the original mesh itself.

mergeclosevert.name: Clean Vertices
mergeclosevert.description: If true, the mesh generated by Marching Cubes will be cleaned by unifying vertices that are almost coincident.

discretize.name: Discretize
discretize.description: If true, intersected edges are placed in fixed middle positions, causing a severe stair-step appearance (aliasing). Useful for simulating 3D printing outputs.

multisample.name: Multi-sample
multisample.description: If true, the distance field is more accurately computed by multisampling the volume. Much slower but causes fewer artifacts.

absdist.name: Absolute Distance
absdist.description: If true, an unsigned distance field is computed. Requires a non-zero Offset to build a double surface around the original.
